PROTECT YOUR SKIN FROM WITHIN

=E2=80=8A

Prepare your skin for the sun

The sun=E2=80=99s rays can make your skin look beautifully fresh and sun-ki=
ssed, but they can also cause redness, inflammation, and sun damage dependi=
ng on how well you prepare your skin. Here are our best tips to help protec=
t your skin this summer!

Why is it important to protect your skin?
Sunlight that reaches the earth is divided into UVA radiation (95% of all s=
unlight) and UVB radiation (5% of all sunlight). UVB radiation stimulates t=
he body=E2=80=99s production of vitamin D. However, overexposure to sunligh=
t can cause significant damage, and you should absolutely avoid getting sun=
burned.=C2=A0Damage from free radicals in the skin includes direct DNA dama=
ge in skin cells, inflammation, reduced production of the skin=E2=80=99s ow=
n antioxidants, and increased production of protease enzymes called matrix =
metalloproteinases (MMPs), which break down collagen and elastin in the der=
mis. Collagen is a protein that helps keep the skin firm and smooth.

[Protect your skin from within with supplements](https://www.greatlife.eu/h=
ealth-goal/skin-nails-hair)

=E2=80=8A

=E2=80=8A

[Living Silica](https://www.greatlife.eu/health-goal/skin-nails-hair)

=E2=80=8A

PROTECT YOUR SKIN WITH SUPPLEMENTS

Our best tips to protect your skin this summer
=C2=A0
Get enough omega-3 fatty acids, especially the long-chain polyunsaturated o=
mega-3s EPA and DHA. EPA and DHA are found in fish oil, among other sources=
. [Wild Alaskan Sockeye Salmon =E2=80=93 Omega-3](https://www.greatlife.eu/=
wild-alaskan-sockeye-salmon-omega-3) is the finest fish oil we have found o=
n the market. The fish oil comes from wild-caught Sockeye salmon harvested =
in the pristine waters of Alaska.

Avoid excessive levels of omega-6. EPA and DHA from Wild Alaskan Sockeye Sa=
lmon =E2=80=93 Omega-3 provide an optimal omega-3 to omega-6 ratio, helping=
 reduce the risk of sunburn during normal sun exposure.

Make sure you get enough antioxidants. The sun=E2=80=99s UV rays create fre=
e radicals in the skin, which can be neutralized when the skin contains suf=
ficient antioxidants. Support your skin=E2=80=99s antioxidant defense from =
within with [vitamin C](https://www.greatlife.eu/vitamin-c-greatlife) or [V=
itamin C PureWay-C=C2=AE](https://www.greatlife.eu/vitamin-c-pureway-greatl=
ife), as well as [copper](https://www.greatlife.eu/copper-gluconate-greatli=
fe), which contributes to normal skin pigmentation.

Drink plenty of plain water. Research shows that higher water intake positi=
vely affects skin physiology.

Protect yourself with clothing or a parasol during prolonged exposure to st=
rong sunlight, or use a high-quality sunscreen. There are more natural and =
less toxic sunscreens with organic ingredients available on the market. Loo=
k for ones with mineral-based UV protection.

[Tan Plus+](https://www.greatlife.eu/tan-plus-greatlife) for beautifully ta=
nned skin. Contains a powerful combination of beta-carotene, turmeric, gree=
n tea extract, and copper, which helps protect the skin against oxidative s=
tress and supports an even, natural-looking tan.

[Buy Tan Plus+](https://www.greatlife.eu/tan-plus-greatlife)

=E2=80=8A

Our advice and tips are not intended for diagnosing or treating diseases. T=
hey do not replace conventional medical care. They are solely provided for =
general information and should not be used as a substitute for professional=
 medical advice, diagnosis, or treatment. Dietary supplements should not be=
 used as a substitute for a varied diet. It is essential to maintain a dive=
rse and balanced diet along with a healthy lifestyle.
